original: Space Shuttle Discovery blasts off on STS-95 with 77-year oldJohn Glennon board, making him the oldest person to go into space.
Bob Ross, American artist and television host, born in Daytona Beach, Florida (d. 1995)
Large complex Maya city Valeriana rediscovered "by accident" using a Lidar laser survey, capable of supporting up to 50,000 people 750-850 AD, in Campeche state, Mexico
Dan Castellaneta, American comedian, and Emmy Award-winning voice actor (The Simpsons -"Homer"), born in Chicago, Illinois
"Asterix" is first published in the French magazine "Pilote" by Rene Goscinny, illustrated byAlbert Uderzo
Golden State shooting guardKlay Thompsonbreaks NBA record previously held by teammate Stephen Curry for most 3-pointers in a game with 14 in Warriors' 149-124 win over the Bulls in Chicago
Gottfried Wilhelm Leibnizmakes the first use of the long s, ∫, for integral, helping discover integral and differential calculus
10th centuryArchimedesPalimpsest, featuring lost works of Archimedes, made freely available on the web, 10 years after bought by unknown tech figure
New research says cacao tree first used in Ecuador by Mayo Chinchipe culture, 5000 years ago, not in central America as previously thought
King Cyrus "the Great" of Persia marches into Babylon, freeing Jewish captives and allowing them to return home
Cassius Clay [Muhammad Ali] in his first professional fight beats Tunney Hunsaker on points in 6 rounds in Louisville, Kentucky
The first ticker-tape parade marking the dedication of the Statue of Liberty is held in New York City
Hurricane Sandy makes landfall in New Jersey resulting in 110 deaths and $50 billion in damage and forces the New York stock exchange to close
Stock market crash on Wall Street, known as "Black Tuesday," triggers the Great Depression
US Supreme Court orders end to all school segregation "at once"
The Who release single "My Generation" in UK
Deadliest Indian Ocean tropical super cyclone hits Odisha, India, death toll 9,885, wind speed of 300 mph
Rising seas could endanger 150 million people worldwide, three times more than previously thought, according study by Climate Central published in "Nature Communications"
China announces the end of its one-child policy after 35 years
First steam-powered warship, "Demologos," is launched in New York for the US Navy
First ballpoint pen goes on sale, manufactured by Reynolds in the US (just beating biro)
World Scrabble Championship won for the fourth time by New Zealand Malaysian Nigel Richards with the word "groutier" (bad-tempered)
Edgar Kaufmann Jr. deeds his family property Fallingwater (1935), designed byFrank Lloyd Wright, to the Western Pennsylvania Conservancy
Parris Goebel, New Zealand dancer and choreographer (Savage x Fenty shows), born in Auckland, New Zealand
Amanda Beard, American swimmer (Olympic gold 100m medley 1996, 200m breaststroke 2004), born in Newport Beach, California
Isao Takahata, Japanese anime film director, screenwriter and producer (Grave of the Fireflies, The Tale of the Princess Kaguya), born in Ujiyamada, Mie, Japan (d. 2018)
American commercial fishing vessel (F/V) 'Andrea Gail' and crew of 6 lost at sea near Sable Island in North Atlantic Ocean; story becomes basis for book and film "The Perfect Storm"
Galt MacDermot, Gerome Ragni and James Rado's hippie musical "Hair" opens off-Broadway at the Public Theatre, NYC, for a limited 6-week run
Ella Fitzgeraldand the Chick Webb Orchestra record "(If You Can't Sing It) You'll Have To Swing It (Mr. Paganini)"; it becomes a staple in her repertoire as a launching point for scat vocals
Gibbs, Grey and Wood's revue "Runnin' Wild" (introducing "The Charleston") opens on Broadway, New York City
Thomas Hearnswins unprecedented 4th different weight boxing title
Eric Staal, Canadian ice hockey center (Stanley Cup 2006 Carolina Hurricanes; World C'ship gold 2007; Winter Olympic gold 2010), born in Thunder Bay, Ontario
Johann Olav Koss, Norwegian speed skater (Olympics 3 gold-1994), born in Drammen, Norway
Primož Roglič, Slovenian road cyclist (Vuelta a España 2019, 20, 21, 24; Giro d'Italia 2023), born in Trbovlje, Yugoslavia
Danish tennis starCaroline Wozniackibeats AmericanVenus Williams6-4, 6-4 to win the WTA Finals title decider in Kallang, Singapore
Record-breaking snowstorm in the northeastern United States leaves nearly 2 million residents without power for more than 36 hours
Alaska highway completed
International Committee of Red Cross forms as result Geneva held conference (Nobel 1917, 1944, 1963)
Special Court of Oyer and Terminer, convened for Salem witch trials, dissolved by the Governor of Massachusetts Bay Colony

Fantasy Records releases "Willy and the Poor Boys", the fourth studio album by the American rock bandCreedence ClearwaterRevival; their third album of the year is their most critically acclaimed, and includes the hits "Down on the Corner", and "Fortunate Son"
Opera "Don Giovanni," with music byWolfgang Amadeus Mozartand a libretto by Lorenzo Da Ponte, premieres at the National Theater of Bohemia in Prague
James Ritty, American saloon owner and inventor (mechanical cash register), born in Dayton, Ohio (d. 1918)[1]
Bill Mauldin, American cartoonist famous for his World War II cartoons depicting American soldiers (Pulitzer-1945, 59), born in Mountain Park, New Mexico (d. 2003)
Ralph Bakshi, Krymchak-American animator and film maker (Fritz the Cat; Lord of the Rings (1978); Mighty Mouse: The New Adventures), born in Haifa, Mandatory Palestine

Neal Hefti, American jazz trumpeter, arranger (Woody Herman; Count Basie); bandleader (Kate Smith Show) and composer ("Li'l Darlin"; The Odd Couple; Batman theme), born in Hastings, Nebraska (d. 2008)
Gabrielle Union, American actress, author and activist (Bring it on, Being Mary Jane), born in Omaha, Nebraska
Tracee Ellis Ross, American actress (Black-ish), born in Los Angeles, California
Ma Huateng [Pony Ma], Chinese entrepreneur (Tencent), born in Chaoyang District, Shantou
Jonathan Brown, Australian rules football forward (AFL Premiership 2001, 02, 03 Brisbane Lions; All-Australian 2007, 09) and broadcaster (Fox Footy, Nova 100), born in Port Fairy, Australia
R. A. Dickey, American baseball pitcher (MLB All-Star, NL Cy Young Award, NL strikeout leader 2012; Gold Glove Award 2013; NY Mets, Toronto Blue Jays), born in Nashville, Tennessee

Michael Carter, American football nose tackle (Super Bowl 1984, 88, 89; Pro Bowl 1985, 87, 88; First-team All-Pro 1986, 87, 88; SF 49ers) and athlete (Olympic silver shot put 1984), born in Dallas, Texas
Randy Jackson, American singer and musician, ninth Jackson brother (Jackson 5 - "ABC"), born in Gary, Indiana
Omara Portuondo, Cuban singer (Cuarteto d'Aida; Buena Vista Social Club), born in Havana, Cuba
Peter Green [Greenbaum], English guitarist (Fleetwood Mac - "Albatross"), born in Bethnal Green, London (d. 2020)[1][2]
Sonny Osborne, American bluegrass banjo player (The Osborne Brothers - "Rocky Top"), born in Roark, Kentucky (d. 2021)
Harriet Powers, African-American slave and quilt maker (Bible Quilt 1886), born in Clarke County, Georgia (d. 1910)
Valérie Masson-Delmotte, French climate scientist (co-chair 2021 report Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change), born in Nancy, France
Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah, Namibian politician, first woman elected President of Namibia (2024), born in Onamutai, South West Africa[1]
Akiko Kojima, Japanese model (Miss Universe 1959 - 1st Japanese and Asian woman to win the title), born in Tokyo
Frank Wykoff, American athlete (Olympic gold 4x100m relay 1928, 32, 36), born in Des Moines, Iowa (d. 1980)
